🚀 Daily AI Digest — June 23, 2026

1️⃣ Five Eyes Warns AI Cyber Attacks "Months Away"
Rare joint intelligence statement from US/UK/Canada/AU/NZ says AI capable of devastating cyber attacks on governments and businesses is months, not years, away.

2️⃣ OpenAI Launches Open-Source Bug Patching Initiative
OpenAI is using its AI to automatically find and patch vulnerabilities in popular open-source projects, addressing chronic underfunding of open-source security.

3️⃣ Groq Raises $650M After Nvidia Deal Collapsed
AI chipmaker Groq confirms massive raise and re-staffing spree after Nvidia's $20B acquisition attempt fell through. LPU tech offers GPU alternative.

4️⃣ Baseten Hits $13B Valuation
AI inference startup reaches $13B in record bet by Australia's Blackbird Ventures. Inference is the new battleground as AI moves to production.

5️⃣ Alphabet Has Worst Day in a Year on AI Fears
Google's parent company sees worst stock drop in 12+ months amid high-profile talent exits and concerns about falling behind in AI race.

6️⃣ AI Catches Heart Condition Doctors Missed
AI flagged a serious heart problem initially diagnosed as asthma. Patient received heart transplant after AI intervention. Diagnostic safety net in action.
